title: oplismenus elatior
intro: Oplismenus elatior, also known as the lesser club sedge, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The leaves and rhizomes of Oplismenus elatior have been used to make herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are believed to offer health benefits including reducing inflammation and aiding in digestion. In addition to its medicinal uses, Oplismenus elatior has been used in traditional folk medicine to treat conditions like bronchitis, coughs, and fever.
